---
title: "3.6 The Danger of Data Errors"
subtitle: "Video lesson slides"
author: "Algorithmic Trading"
notes: |
  Mean-reversion strategies are especially vulnerable to bad ticks because they
  interpret one outlier print as a bargain. The resulting backtest profit can be
  entirely fictitious, and the same logic can trigger losing live trades.

format:
  revealjs:
    theme: default
    css: ../../../../styles/reveal.css
    slide-number: true
    controls: true
    progress: true
    menu: false
    transition: slide
    transition-speed: fast
    center: false
    auto-stretch: true
    auto-animate: true
    show-notes: false
    scrollable: true
    embed-resources: false
filters:
  - ../../../../scripts/lua/remove-reveal-notes.lua
include-before-body:
  - ../../../../styles/reveal-audience-only.html
execute:
  echo: true
---

## One bad tick can create a fake mean-reversion profit that never existed.

| True path              | Corrupted path                        |
| ---------------------- | ------------------------------------- |
| 100, 100, 100          | 100, 110, 100                         |
| no trade opportunity   | fake short and fake profit            |

::: {.notes}
Open with the simplest possible example. Mean reversion sees the bad tick as a
temporary spike, enters, and appears to make money when the error disappears.
:::

## Spread strategies amplify data errors because the signal is a small difference of large prices.

| Quote levels           | True spread | Error in one leg | Distorted spread |
| ---------------------- | ----------- | ---------------- | ---------------- |
| 100 and 105            | 5           | +1               | 6                |

::: {.notes}
This is the key reason pair trading is especially fragile. A one-dollar quote
error is only a 1 percent price error on one leg, but a 20 percent error on a
five-dollar spread.
:::

## Backtest inflation and live execution loss come from the same bad tick mechanism.

::: {.notes}
Close by making the risk symmetric. The same error that creates fake historical
alpha can trigger a real losing live order.
:::
